title: Dispatcher: fix log file date — UTC vs CEST timezone mismatch
description:
Dispatcher log files are named by `new Date().toISOString().slice(0, 10)` (UTC date). But the systemd service runs in a CEST environment (UTC+2). Log entries written between 00:00–01:59 CEST appear in the previous day's file because that time window is still UTC the previous day.

This is a cosmetic issue (no data loss, entries are correctly timestamped) but causes confusion when tracing overnight dispatcher behavior — e.g. a 01:30 CEST event appears to have happened the day before.

Fix options (pick one):
A. Name files by local date: `new Date().toLocaleDateString('sv')` (ISO format, locale-independent)
B. Document it in a comment above the `filePath` line in `appendLog()` so future maintainers know

Option B is the minimal-risk fix given logs are rolling anyway. Option A is cleaner.

File: `workspace/operations/bin/task-dispatcher.mjs`, function `appendLog()`, line that builds `filePath`.
handoff_notes: Changed appendLog() to use new Date().toLocaleDateString('sv') for log file naming. Swedish locale (sv) produces ISO date format (YYYY-MM-DD) from local time, so midnight CEST no longer causes entries to land in the previous UTC day's file.
